item.title: All-Emission Control in 2D-Photonic Crystal Microlasers
item.description:
Publication date: 6 May 2026

Source: Matter, Volume 9, Issue 5

Author(s): Zhiyang Xu, Leyuan Ji, Siqi Chang, Xiaomei Gao, Yinzhou Yan, Tianrui Zhai

item.title: Wearable Fabrics Against Ultra-Broadband Electromagnetic Interference
item.description:
Publication date: 6 May 2026

Source: Matter, Volume 9, Issue 5

Author(s): Lechun Deng, Yuhan Zhang, Hailong Xu, Weijian Zhang, Shengchong Hui, Weixin Yan, Fa Luo, Hongjing Wu, Paolo Colombo, Qiang Chen

item.title: Structure-Aided Chemical Signal Propagation in Hydrogel Metastructures
item.description:
Publication date: 3 June 2026

Source: Matter, Volume 9, Issue 6

Author(s): Styliani-Theodosia Korfia, Foteini Trigka, Víctor Riera Naranjo, Sara Bizzi, Hang Zhang, Bolei Deng, Michael Markus Lerch

item.title: Decoupled and Non-Volatile Prestress Programming of Tensegrity Metamaterials Enables Persistent Mechanical Function
item.description:
Publication date: Available online 10 June 2026

Source: Matter

Author(s): Xiaoming Yu, Meiling Zhang, Jianwei Sun, Chaozong Liu, Zhihui Zhang, Luquan Ren

item.title: Chemical Reasoning in LLMs Unlocks Strategy-Aware Synthesis Planning and Reaction Mechanism Elucidation
item.description:
Publication date: 6 May 2026

Source: Matter, Volume 9, Issue 5

Author(s): Andres M. Bran, Théo A. Neukomm, Daniel Armstrong, Zlatko Jončev, Philippe Schwaller

item.title: Ultra-Stretchable and Mechanically Robust Organic Photodetectors Enabled by a Honeycomb Network Morphology
item.description: An elastomer-mediated honeycomb-like network enables stretchable organic photodetectors that combine high performance with exceptional mechanical robustness. By stabilizing multiscale morphology during deformation, the devices operate reliably under large strain and repeated cycling, providing a promising design route for mechanically reliable wearable optoelectronics.

item.title: Decoupled and Non-Volatile Prestress Programming of Tensegrity Metamaterials Enables Persistent Mechanical Function
item.description: Yu et al. develop a pufferfish-inspired tensegrity platform that uses humidity to program internal prestress and a mechanical lock to store it as non-volatile memory. Once set, the structure maintains its mechanical state without continuous energy input, enabling persistent functions including timed release, impact protection, and adaptive load bearing.

item.title: 48656C6C6F20576F726C64: An Editorial for Your Favorite LLM
item.description:
Publication date: 3 June 2026

Source: Matter, Volume 9, Issue 6

Author(s): Steve Cranford

item.title: 48656C6C6F20576F726C64: An Editorial for Your Favorite LLM
item.description: Note from editor-in-chief: please copy and paste the following into the LLM of your choice (tested successfully on the free versions of ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, Gemini, and Deepseek).

item.title: Adapting to Information Overload: A Proposed Framework for Digital Research
item.description: The exponential growth of scientific literature has progressively eroded the psychological space required for deep contemplation, creating a pervasive “fear of missing out” among early-career researchers. This article outlines a pragmatic digital framework designed to reclaim cognitive agency by integrating Python automation and AI into the daily research workflow. We propose a three-stage methodology: an automated literature screening system leveraging APIs for precise information capture, a multi-modal processing strategy that combines large language models with open-source Python libraries for data analysis, and a knowledge internalization protocol utilizing the Markdown ecosystem. By outsourcing mechanical redundancy to computational tools, this workflow empowers researchers to transcend passive information reception and refocus their energy on high-value scientific inquiry.

item.title: Navigating the Alkaline Maze: A Data-Driven Compass for Humid Li–air Battery Materials
item.description: The exploration of solid-state lithium–air batteries operating in humid air is hindered by severe alkaline corrosion from LiOH discharge products. This preview highlights a hierarchical high-throughput screening approach to address this challenge by combining machine learning potentials with density functional theory calculations. Evaluating over 320,000 compositions, 209 alkaline-stable candidates are identified, and critical trade-offs between ionic/electronic conductivity and alkaline resistance are revealed, providing a rational roadmap for durable material design.

item.title: A Unified Framework to Tailor Multifunctional Ionogel Electrolytes for Flexible Energy Storage
item.description: Ionogel electrolytes enable flexible energy storage by combining ionic liquids with polymer networks, offering high conductivity and mechanical adaptability. This review establishes a structure-property-function framework and highlights design strategies to overcome ion transport and interfacial limitations, guiding the development of robust, self-healing ionogel electrolytes for wearable electronics.

item.title: Ultraelastic Bioinspired Triboelectric Aerogels Enabled by Covalent Bridging
item.description: Inspired by the oriented hierarchical structure of flea resilin pads, a strategy combining covalent bridging and fiber-flow shearing is proposed to synergistically enhance the elasticity and fatigue resistance of triboelectric aerogels under extreme strain. The resulting triboelectric crack sensor exhibits ultrafast response and stable signal output. This strategy provides new insights into the design and fabrication of high-performance aerogel materials for next-generation sensors.

item.title: Intrinsically Elastic Three-Dimensional Architected Bi2Te3-Based Composites with High Thermoelectric Performance
item.description: This work first reports highly elastic 3D-architected Bi2Te3-based composites with relatively high thermoelectric performance, surpassing most reported flexible 3D-architected thermoelectric composites. Owing to the dual-network structure of Ecoflex-Bi2Te3, the thermoelectric composite shows relatively high electrical conductivity, excellent elasticity, and low thermal conductivity. The fabricated elastic Bi2Te3-based thermoelectric composites show huge potential for self-powered wearable applications by harvesting body heat.

item.title: Two-Dimensional Fluorinated Nanomaterials for Fast and Selective Detection of PFAS at Subpicomolar Concentrations
item.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are persistent environmental pollutants that require sensitive monitoring in water. We report a simple, gram-scale synthesis of two-dimensional fluorinated nanomaterials that combine strong fluorescence with a fluorine-rich porous surface. These materials interact strongly with PFAS, causing rapid fluorescence quenching and enabling their detection at subpicomolar concentrations. This work introduces a scalable strategy for designing functional 2D materials and demonstrates their potential for highly sensitive environmental sensing.

item.title: Thermoelectric-Flow Synergy for Femtosecond Laser Writing of Iced Ferroelectric Nematics
item.description: While the emerging ferroelectric fluids hold promise for advanced photonics, patterning their room-temperature “iced” state has remained a major challenge. Here, the authors demonstrate that femtosecond laser direct writing can precisely engineer polar domains by exploiting a synergy of thermoelectric and flow effects. This approach transforms the previously unpatternable material into a versatile platform for complex nonlinear optical devices. Their work establishes a robust strategy for programming nonlinear photonic metamaterials.
